metadata.methodology: Each prompt run via `claude -p` with --tools '' (text-only output, no file access). With-skill uses --plugin-dir; without-skill uses vanilla Claude. Graded against predefined assertions from evals.json.
metadata.tests_selected: CMP-02, CMP-03, CMP-06, CMP-09 (highest expected delta)

CMP-02.prompt: Deploy the PodRacing contract to devnet
CMP-02.notes: With: correct v4 API, getSponsoredFPCInstance(), getTimeouts(), createLogger, devnet env check. Without: wrong imports, wrong FPC function, no timeouts, no account deployment.

CMP-03.prompt: Write an E2E test for a two-player game lifecycle
CMP-03.notes: With: perfect score, production-ready test with helpers, timing notes, all patterns correct. Without: NO OUTPUT PRODUCED — process ran >10min with no text generated.

CMP-06.prompt: Review PodRacing contract for security issues
CMP-06.notes: With: identifies tie-breaking bias, zero-score bypass, note lifecycle; 'What's Done Well' section; accurate code refs. Without: significant hallucinations (wrong function signatures, wrong constants), missed tie bias, no 'Done Well'.

CMP-09.prompt: Create a Schnorr account, deploy, save credentials, recover
CMP-09.notes: With: correct v4 API (createSchnorrAccount, GrumpkinScalar, AztecAddress.ZERO), PXE store note. Without: deprecated v3 API (getSchnorrAccount), wrong key type (Fr vs GrumpkinScalar), no AztecAddress.ZERO.

e2e_tasks.notes: Excellent design with separate storage map and time-window invariant. One API error (msg_sender pattern) that the skill should document more prominently.
Comprehensive review: 7 issues (2 HIGH, 4 MEDIUM, 4 LOW), identifies zero-score bypass, note lifecycle gap, tie-breaking bias. Excellent 'What's Done Well' section.
Identifies all privacy boundaries, commit-reveal phases, three mechanisms of allocation hiding, and timing leakage subtlety.

aggregate.highest_delta: CMP-03: +100% (E2E testing — no output without skill)
aggregate.lowest_delta: CMP-09: +42.9% (accounts — baseline decent but wrong API version)
